pages.title: Daniel Zeichner
headings: h2: Advice and Assistance for Constituents
pages.text_excerpt: Daniel Zeichner Skip to main content Daniel Zeichner Standing up for Cambridge Contact About More Menu Contact Daniel You can contact Daniel Zeichner MP by phone, email or post. Call: 020 7219 4379 (answerphone service) Email: [email protected] Post: House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Advice and Assistance for Constituents Surgeries Daniel holds regular surgeries where Cambridge constituents can discuss issues with him that they need help with. These sessions are currently held online and by appointment only. You can find out if Daniel is your MP by entering your postcode here: https://members.parliament.uk/findyourmp If you reside in the Cambridge constituency and need assistance, please contact Daniel at [email protected] or by telephone (answerphone) on 020 7219 4379. Due to the volume of enquiries received, there may be a slight delay in responding. What is Casework? Casework refers to requests for assistance that constituents bring to Daniel. This can range from urgent matters like imminent homelessness to ongoing issues with welfare payments. Daniel’s team of caseworkers is dedicated to handling each case fairly and compassionately. Depending on the urgency, they aim to respond promptly and may request further details or schedule a meeting with Daniel at one of his constituency surgeries. Prioritisation of Casework Casework is prioritised based on urgency: Urgent Casework: Time-critical issues such as imminent homelessness. Standard Casework: Important issues that may require up to 10 working days to address. Policy Casework: Involving national or local policy enquiries that may require extensive research. Who Can Daniel Help? Daniel assists residents within the Cambridge constituency. Please ensure you provide your full name , postal address , and contact telephone number when getting in touch. This helps us efficiently allocate resources to assist you. Issues Daniel Can Assist With Daniel can assist with matters falling under the jurisdiction of various government departments including: Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) HM Revenue & Customs (HMRC) Home Office Foreign, Commonwealth, & Development Office Department of Health & Social Care Department for Education For issues not under central government responsibility, such as those involving the city or county councils, or the combined authority, please contact your local councillor in the first instance. Exclusions Daniel cannot provide: Legal advice or interference with legal proceedings Consumer or contract advice Debt advice or financial services guidance Mediation in personal disputes Respectful Engagement Daniel and his team are committed to providing meaningful assistance and ask that all communication be conducted respectfully. Abuse or threats towards Daniel or his team will not be tolerated. Campaigns, Policy, and Lobbying Daniel welcomes hearing from constituents about campaigns, policy issues, and lobbying efforts. He considers requests to support campaigns or to participate in events, whether in the constituency or in Westminster. Freedom of Information and Data Protection As a Member of Parliament, Daniel is not covered by the Freedom of Information Act. However, under the Data Protection Act, constituents have the right to access information held by his office without charge. Useful Contacts: Addenbrooke’s Hospital: https://www.cuh.nhs.uk/contact-us/ Main hospital switchboard: 01223 805000 Anglia Ruskin Family Law Clinic – for free legal advice: https://www.aru.ac.uk/business-and-law/economics-finance-and-law/law-clinic Anglia Ruskin Student Support: https://aru.ac.uk/student-life/support-and-facilities/student-services Benefits, Tax, DVLA, TV Licensing: https://www.gov.uk/ Cambridge City Council: http://www.cambridge.gov.uk/ Main switchboard 01223 457000 Out-of-hours emergencies: 0300 303 8389 Cambridge Ethnic Community Forum: https://cecf.co.uk/ Cambridge Money Advice Centre – offers free, confidential advice to help you get out and stay out of debt

pages.title: Daniel Zeichner
headings: h2: Cambridge is a great city.
pages.text_excerpt: Daniel Zeichner Skip to main content Daniel Zeichner Standing up for Cambridge Contact About More Menu Cambridge is a great city. Local residents are proud to live, study and work here. Over the last decade, Daniel Zeichner has built a reputation for being a trusted Member of Parliament. He is a committed environmentalist, he stands up for our public services and he always supports Cambridge United. The Labour Government has a plan to reboot our economy, revitalise our public services and green our country. It won’t be easy, in fact it will be hard, and there will be tough choices and trade-offs to be made. Daniel is proud to be a Labour MP working with a Labour Government to bring about lasting change. Daniel Zeichner MP Daniel’s values have been consistent since he first moved to Cambridge to study at King’s College. As our MP, Daniel has always been a champion for Cambridge: Committed environmentalist: Daniel’s politics are red and green. Public services matter: Daniel has fought privatisation of our health services, and campaigned for fairer funding for local schools. Living wage champion: Daniel believes in social justice and is a recognised Parliamentary Living Wage Champion. Students: Daniel works to make studying in Cambridge an amazing experience with access to support, including better mental health provision. Constitutional reformer: Decades ago with the late Robin Cook, Daniel began campaigning for a democratically elected House of Lords. Now it is in Labour’s manifesto. Making Cambridge the best small city in the world: Everyone has heard about Cambridge but past success is no guarantee for the future. Daniel works tirelessly to make sure our city is a great place to live, study, work and do business. Daniel Zeichner Contact details House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA '> View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Balint Ferenczi on behalf of Daniel Zeichner, both at Alex Wood Hall, Norfolk Street, Cambridge, CB1 2LD. ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
